exemplar.title: How is narrative voice used to portray the theme of infatuation in The Virgin Suicides by Jeffrey Eugenides?

detailedExemplarData.title: How is narrative voice used to portray the theme of infatuation in The Virgin Suicides by Jeffrey Eugenides?

subject.title: English A Lit
subject.description:
**IB English A Literature** is a **Group 1** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **literary texts from diverse cultures, genres, and time periods**, with a strong focus on **critical analysis, interpretation, and understanding of how literature reflects and shapes human experience through themes, cultural contexts, and global issues**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Unseen literary text analysis | 1h 15m | 2h 15m | 35% |
| **Paper 2** | Comparative essay on studied works | 1h 45m | 1h 45m | 40% |
| **Individual Oral** | Oral presentation on global issues | 10 minutes | 10 minutes | 25% |

strengths: Research question is clearly stated, focused, and consistently connected to the discussion.
Introduction and section headings maintain a clear purpose and focus on narrative voice and infatuation.
Topic is communicated accurately and effectively throughout, with no major digressions.
weaknesses: Methodology section lacks sufficient detail on source selection criteria, bias mitigation, and systematic data collection.
Literature review does not clearly tie each secondary source back to the research question.
Some analysis sections (e.g., fragmented memories) need more explicit linkage to the methodological framework.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two of the three strands (topic clarity and research question focus) demonstrably meet the 5-6 band, but the methodology strand is only at the 3-4 band due to missing procedural rigor. On balance, the overall performance in Focus and Method aligns with the 3-4 band descriptor, meriting 4 marks.

strengths: Terminology such as âmale gaze,â âunreliable narrator,â and âreligious imageryâ is used accurately and consistently.
Close readings of key scenes (e.g., forbidden lipstick, suicide invitation) demonstrate solid understanding of the text.
Some insightful symbolic interpretations (choir-robe imagery) show coherent grasp of literary concepts.
weaknesses: The range of secondary sources is narrow and non-peer-reviewed, limiting the depth of scholarly engagement.
Over-reliance on paraphrase and plot summary in places reduces the effectiveness of source application.
Introduction contains excessive narrative description at the expense of analytical depth.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the use of subject-specific terminology meets the 5-6 band, knowledge of topic and sources is only at the 3-4 band due to limited and partially applied scholarship. The overall Knowledge and Understanding performance sits in the 3-4 band, justifying 3 marks.

strengths: The essay maintains a coherent, text-embedded argument that consistently ties back to the research question.
Analysis of key passages is supported by well-chosen quotations and logical progression of ideas.
Sustained attention to narrative voice demonstrates competent analytical depth.
weaknesses: Research draws primarily on a small set of popular criticism, lacking scholarly breadth and critical comparison.
Evaluation of alternative viewpoints and methodological limitations is superficial.
Engagement with theoretical frameworks (unreliable narrator, feminist theory) is underdeveloped.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strands for research relevance and evaluation of arguments fall in the 4-6 band, while analysis is at the lower end of the 7-9 band. Overall, the Critical Thinking performance aligns with the middle of the 4-6 band, warranting 5 out of 12 marks.

strengths: Overall structure is clearly organized with appropriate section headings that reinforce the analytic focus.
All required components (title, bibliography, labeled sections) are present, aiding navigation.
weaknesses: Page numbers are missing and heading levels are inconsistently applied.
Layout lacks uniform formatting (bibliography indents, punctuation), and there are proofreading oversights.
Some paragraphs are densely packed, reducing readability.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the structural elements fulfill the 3-4 band, the inconsistent layout and formatting issues place presentation in the 1-2 band overall. Therefore, 2 out of 4 marks are awarded.

strandSelections.reasoning: The methodology outlines rereading the primary text, selecting passages, and consulting secondary criticism via JSTOR, with brief mention of credibility checks. **Basic procedures and selection criteria are described, but there is limited detail about alternative critical frameworks, bias mitigation, or systematic data-recording.** The narrow range of only three secondary sources further signals partial completeness. These features align with a competent yet not fully developed methodology, meriting the 3-4 band.

Basic layout conventionsâtitle, word count and bibliographyâare met, but **page numbers are missing, heading hierarchy is inconsistent, and bibliography lacks hanging indents and uniform punctuation**. These flaws indicate that layout considerations are only partially and inconsistently applied, corresponding to the 1-2 band; a score of 2 recognises that some elements are correct while notable lapses remain.

The student undertakes **reasonable independent research**, but the corpus is small and draws largely on popular criticism rather than scholarly, peer-reviewed work. Still, each source is relevant and integrated into the discussion, showing a clear (if limited) connection to the RQ. The research thus rises above âminimalâ but does not attain the comprehensive range or depth required for the top bands.

The student opens with a concise, engaging synopsis of the novel, immediately signalling the focus on narrative voice and infatuation. The link between narrative technique (first-person plural, male gaze) and the thematic concern is **explicitly articulated**, and every subsequent section title reiterates the same line of inquiry. There is no ambiguity about scope (one novel, one literary device, one theme), and peripheral material is generally avoided. Despite a few minor digressions into plot summary, the topic remains clear and purposeful throughout, satisfying the descriptor for the top band.

The essay constructs a **coherent overall argument** that returns to the RQ in each major section, demonstrating logical progression. Evaluation of alternative viewpoints, however, is brief, and discussions of source limitations or counter-claims are under-developed, leading to some uncritical acceptance of secondary ideas. As a result, critical evaluation is present but only partial, consistent with the middle (4-6) band; a score of 5 reflects generally sound but not sustained evaluative engagement.

The analysis offers **sustained, text-embedded readings** of key passages, unpacking diction, imagery and narrative stance to illuminate the boysâ objectification of the sisters. Claims are usually supported by quotation, and paragraphing follows a clear analytical logic. Nevertheless, some interpretations drift into extended description (e.g., recounting Luxâs sexuality) and moments of overstatement, preventing the consistent sophistication required for the 10-12 band. The work sits solidly in the 7-9 range, meriting a mid-level 7.

The report follows EE conventions (title page, abstract not required, clearly labelled sections, logical flow from introduction to conclusion). **Headings and sub-headings aid navigation, and the bibliography is present.** Minor omissions (e.g., some abrupt transitions) do not undermine the overall appropriateness. All required structural elements are present, satisfying the 3-4 band; the clarity justifies the top of that band.

The essay demonstrates **solid understanding of the novel and its key critical issues** (male gaze, unreliable narration, religious symbolism). Primary-text quotation and close analysis are generally apt. However, knowledge of secondary scholarship is restricted to three journalistic or web-based essays, none peer-reviewed, limiting depth and breadth. Occasional over-reliance on paraphrase and unsupported assertions (e.g., religion as âpunishmentâ) show that application of sources is only partially effective. These features fit comfortably within the 3-4 band.

Throughout, the student employs **accurate, discipline-specific terminology**ââmale gaze,â âunreliable narrator,â âfirst-person plural perspective,â âobjectification,â âreligious imageryââand applies these concepts consistently to textual evidence. Terminology is used purposefully to advance the argument rather than decorate it, demonstrating sophisticated conceptual control. This matches the expectations of the 5-6 band.

The research questionââHow is narrative voice used to portray the theme of infatuation in The Virgin Suicides?ââis **precise, focused and directly drives the essayâs structure**. Each analytical section (narrative voice, male gaze, Luxâs sexuality, religious imagery) returns explicitly to explaining how narrative voice constructs infatuation. Signposting in topic sentences and the conclusion shows a sustained, effective connection between discussion and the RQ, fully aligning with the 5-6 band.

annotations.comment: The current secondary sources are primarily journalistic; incorporation of peer-reviewed scholarship would broaden the depth and credibility of the evidence base.
The research question is stated clearly and precisely, providing a strong focal point for the investigation and guiding the structure of the essay effectively.
The inclusion of the word count at the start meets formatting requirements and demonstrates adherence to formal report conventions.
Page numbering is absent from the footer, which impedes easy navigation and does not fully satisfy formatting expectations.
The introduction presents a clear overview of the text and establishes the thematic focus on narrative voice and infatuation with immediate clarity and purpose.
Heading levels are inconsistent (h3 here versus h2 earlier); maintain a clear hierarchy to support logical structure.
The methodology section outlines basic procedures but lacks detail on source selection criteria, bias mitigation, and systematic data recording to demonstrate rigour.
The introduction relies heavily on plot summary, limiting the space for analytical insight; consider trimming narrative description to emphasize critical interpretation.
The section heading is well-crafted and recurrently reinforces the analytic focus on narrative voiceâs impact on the story.
Consider adding peer-reviewed journal articles to the bibliography to enhance the academic depth and credibility of the research.
The contextualization of the suicide invitation scene as a subversion of the boysâ objectification is perceptive and well-argued.
The analysis of Luxâs sexuality should more explicitly tie back to the research question on narrative voice and infatuation.
First-person phrasing (âmy essayâ) reduces formality; revise to maintain an objective academic tone throughout.
The literature review is clearly signposted with an appropriate heading, aiding reader navigation and enhancing structural coherence.
The review summarises sources but does not critically engage with their differing perspectives or methodologies; deeper evaluative comparison would strengthen the discussion.
The literature review does not explicitly tie each source back to the research question; make clear how each article informs analysis of narrative voice and infatuation.
The symbolic reading of the forbidden lipstick as a manifestation of objectification is insightful and well-supported by textual evidence.
This paragraph is densely packed and would be more readable if split into two focused analyses.
Precise use of terminology such as âfirst-person plural perspectiveâ and âbiased point-of-viewâ demonstrates solid command of literary concepts.
The examination of the boysâ invasive behaviour and its link to power dynamics effectively illustrates the objectifying male gaze.
The discussion of fragmented memories would benefit from explicit connection to how these narrative gaps support the theme of infatuation.
Assertions on narrator reliability would be strengthened by referencing critical theory (e.g., scholarship on the unreliable narrator).
The gendered assumptions lack engagement with feminist theory; consider integrating key critical perspectives on femininity and gaze.
A concise synthesis connects the critique of narrative voice with the oversimplification of Luxâs character, strengthening the evaluative argument.
A sentence fragment at the start indicates a proofreading oversight; ensure all sentences are complete and grammatically correct.
The discussion of Luxâs âcarnal angelâ portrayal would be enhanced by engaging with feminist theories of sexuality and representation.
Bibliography entries lack hanging indents and consistent punctuation; format references uniformly for professional presentation.
The analysis of the choir-robe imagery effectively reveals how religious symbolism reinforces the theme of purity and infatuation.
The conclusion heading is clear and signals a cohesive wrap-up of the essayâs arguments.
The conclusion restates key points but omits reflection on methodological limitations and suggestions for further research.
The bibliography section is clearly labeled, facilitating easy reference to source material.
Citation styles vary between entries; adopt and apply a single referencing format (MLA, APA, etc.) for consistency.
